goal: Fix pipeline auto-continue so directives don't die at step boundaries

risks: Step-loop instruction may not survive context limits — LLM can still be forced to stop. Stalled detection is the real safety net.
Stalled detection heuristic may false-positive on directives waiting for CEO approval. Need to exclude approve/completion steps.
Project.json persistence is already partially instructed in 09-execute-projects.md — reinforcement in the right place is enough.
over_engineering_flags: Do NOT build auto-resume that spawns CLI sessions. Detection-only is the 80% value.
Don't restructure the entire execute doc for project.json persistence.

projects.title: Step-loop, stalled detection, and project.json persistence
projects.scope_summary: Three changes: (1) Add step-loop block to SKILL.md instructing LLM to continue to next step after each completion. (2) Add stalled-directive detection to directive-watcher.ts — flag directives where all tasks are done but pipeline hasn't advanced, excluding CEO action steps. (3) Add instruction in 09-execute-projects.md to verify project.json task statuses after each task.
